assertions.description: Initial state shows two empty grids side-by-side (Q² and R²) with target zone at (√2, √2) marked — the problem is posed: what happens to a Cauchy sequence in different spaces?
Clicking 'Add Next Point' adds the next rational approximation to √2
Both grids show the SAME sequence, but with different outcomes: Q² shows 'Limit not in Q²!' while R² shows 'Converges to (√2, √2) ✓'
The sequence gets arbitrarily close to (√2, √2) but that point only exists in R², not Q²
Student discovers that the SAME Cauchy sequence has completely different fates depending on the ambient space — completeness is about whether limits exist, not about sequence behavior
assertions.verification: After adding 5+ points, left panel shows red 'Limit not in Q²!' while right panel shows green 'Converges' message
